motivation.why_caregiving: I lost my mother to cancer and the hospice nurse showed me what this work can mean.
motivation.proudest_placement: A gentleman with ALS asked me to be there when he passed. It was peaceful.

motivation.why_caregiving: My grandmother had Alzheimer's and I wanted to give other families those moments too.
motivation.proudest_placement: A former surgeon who lost speech. We played chess every afternoon.

motivation.why_caregiving: ICU showed me the most meaningful moments happen in quiet times.
motivation.proudest_placement: Post-stroke patient who said my name for the first time after six months.

motivation.why_caregiving: I cared for my grandmother through her Alzheimer's and found my calling.
motivation.ideal_client: Elderly clients who need patience and consistency.

motivation.why_caregiving: My husband had a stroke. I turned my personal loss into a career.
After my kids left home, I wanted purpose. Live-in care gave me a home and family.
motivation.proudest_placement: Lived with a couple for two years. We became like family.
motivation.ideal_client: Couples where one needs dementia care.

motivation.why_caregiving: I wanted work where I could talk to people, not just do tasks.
motivation.proudest_placement: A lonely diabetic client. Crossword puzzles helped her recover.
motivation.ideal_client: Seniors who need help but mostly need company.

motivation.why_caregiving: Hospital LPN felt like a task machine. Home care lets me build relationships.
motivation.proudest_placement: Post-stroke patient. He's still home a year later.

motivation.why_caregiving: My cousin has cerebral palsy. The work is about helping people do for themselves.
motivation.proudest_placement: A bariatric client who gave up on going outside.
motivation.ideal_client: Clients working on recovery who want independence.

motivation.why_caregiving: My brother has autism. Kids with special needs just want to be kids.
motivation.proudest_placement: A nonverbal child with brain injury. Art brought her out of her shell.

a73d4247-b2b2-498e-8f90-b619040371ee.name_for_reference: David Kim - PSW with OT Background

motivation.why_caregiving: OT assistant background. Home care lets me help people actually live better.
motivation.proudest_placement: Parkinson's client. Falls dropped 80% with home modifications.
motivation.ideal_client: Clients with mobility challenges who want to stay safe.

motivation.why_caregiving: Everyone deserves company, not just care.
motivation.proudest_placement: A client whose family lived far away. I became her connection to the world.
motivation.ideal_client: Seniors who need help getting out and staying social.

motivation.why_caregiving: My father had dementia. I know what families go through.
motivation.proudest_placement: A client with diabetes. Her A1C dropped with consistent meal planning.
motivation.ideal_client: Elderly clients who need consistent, reliable care.

motivation.why_caregiving: I enjoy helping people recover and get back on their feet.
motivation.proudest_placement: Post-hospital recovery client. She regained full mobility.

motivation.why_caregiving: I'm studying nursing and want practical experience in home care.
motivation.ideal_client: Anyone who needs a caring, eager learner.
